meaning: n.改写的草稿,反汇票vt.改写,重新起草
examples: Redraft, rewrite, start again... Work consistently and often.
Now that we have more information, I think we should redraft our original plan.
Immediately following that decision, Finance Minister Pierre Moscovici said his government would redraft a similar proposal.
The Commission will now have to redraft the 2013 budget plan, and amended proposals are due to be published by the end of November.
However, should the House redraft the legislation, it could--and is likely to--allow some of the Senate tax provisions to be slipped in.
